response.justification: The role requires 8–12 years of deep C++ development with STL, multi-threading, industrial protocols, and IEC 62443 secure-development-lifecycle experience. Bogdan's C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis side project; his career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web-frontend technologies. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, MQTT or OPC UA usage, SDL/IEC 62443 practice, or Azure cloud work, making the candidate a poor match for the core requirements of this position.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, event-driven micro-services with async I/O and concurrency (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby), showing latent awareness of performance-critical systems
Polyglot background spanning DLang, Java, C#, embedded/hardware work with robotic arms, and GIS platform development, indicating adaptability across technical paradigms
Strong TDD, CI/CD, agile craftsmanship practices with a visible mentoring track record (Code Katas, Selenium course, conference presentations)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible for a senior C++ role: approximately six months at NI plus a thesis project, with no mention of STL depth, modern C++ standards, or multi-threaded systems design
Zero industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, or OT-protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104), which are explicit core and advantageous requirements
Active career gap since October 2025 combined with no Azure exposure adds compounding re-skilling risk on top of an already substantial technical mismatch
